title: The Democracy Satisfaction Paradox
alt_text: Data visualization: voter turnout vs democratic satisfaction — Switzerland low turnout, highest satisfaction worldwide
slides:
Switzerland: 45% voter turnout. 83% satisfied with democracy.

The US: 55% turnout. 30% satisfaction.

Voting more doesn't mean feeling heard more.

Belgium has compulsory voting — 89% turnout. Satisfaction: middling.
Switzerland votes voluntarily — 45% turnout. Satisfaction: highest in the world.

The variable isn't how often people vote. It's whether voting changes anything.

Swiss citizens vote on 4–6 national referendums per year. On specific laws. Spending decisions. Constitutional questions.

If you don't like something, you can put it to a vote. That's a structurally different relationship with democracy.
sources: IDEA Voter Turnout Database · World Values Survey 2022
